solver: D-Wave (quantum annealer / LeapHybridSampler / neal simulated annealer)

models.description: Single-track railway rescheduling (dispatching) formulated as a QUBO/HOBO for quantum annealing. The underlying integer-linear delay-assignment problem of Domino, Kundu, Salehi & Krawiec (Quantum Inf. Process. 21:337, 2022) assigns each train a discrete secondary delay at each station so as to minimize a weighted sum of delays subject to operational constraints (sum-to-one, minimal headway, single-track, minimal stay, rolling-stock circulation, switch occupancy, track occupancy). The constraints are not stored as linear (in)equalities; each is converted into a squared/penalty term written directly into a symmetric Q matrix (make_Qubo). The track-occupancy condition is originally cubic (HOBO) and is reduced to quadratic via a Rosenberg decomposition that introduces auxiliary binary variables. NO classical CPLEX/docplex/pulp/gurobipy model is present in this repository.

sets_indices.name: S_j
sets_indices.meaning: ordered path (sequence of stations) of train j

sets_indices.name: idx
sets_indices.meaning: primary variable index set: triples (j,s,d) with j in J, s in S_j (non-skipped), d in {0,...,d_max}; one binary per triple

sets_indices.name: idx_z
sets_indices.meaning: auxiliary (Rosenberg) variable index set: tuples (j,j1,s,d,d1) over train pairs sharing a track at station s

sets_indices.name: Jd
sets_indices.meaning: trains ordered by departure on segment (s -> s_next), used for headway pairs

sets_indices.name: Josingle
sets_indices.meaning: train pairs travelling opposite directions on a single-track line between (s,s1)

sets_indices.name: Jround
sets_indices.meaning: rolling-stock circulation (turnaround) train pairs at a station

sets_indices.name: Jtrack
sets_indices.meaning: train groups sharing the same track (station) for the track-occupancy condition

sets_indices.name: Jswitch
sets_indices.meaning: train pairs whose paths share a common switch s

parameters.name: d_max
parameters.meaning: maximal admissible secondary delay (caps the delay index range)

parameters.name: tau^{pass}
parameters.meaning: passing (running) time of a train between two stations

parameters.name: tau^{headway}
parameters.meaning: minimal headway between two trains on a segment

parameters.name: tau^{stop}
parameters.meaning: minimal stop (dwell) time at a station

parameters.name: tau^{prep}
parameters.meaning: minimal preparation (turnaround) time before a rolling-stock circulation departure

parameters.name: tau^{res}
parameters.meaning: switch reservation/blocking time (resource separation at a switch)

parameters.name: t^{(min)}_{j,s}
parameters.meaning: earliest possible departure time of train j from station s (timetable + unavoidable delay)

parameters.name: w_{j,s}
parameters.meaning: penalty weight for a delay above the unavoidable one for train j at station s (objective coefficient)

parameters.name: p_sum
parameters.meaning: Lagrange penalty multiplier for the sum-to-one hard constraint

parameters.name: p_pair
parameters.meaning: Lagrange penalty multiplier for quadratic (pairwise) hard constraints

parameters.name: p_qubic
parameters.meaning: Lagrange penalty multiplier for the Rosenberg decomposition of the cubic track-occupancy term

decision_variables.name: x_{j,s,d}
decision_variables.meaning: 1 if train j departs station s with secondary delay d (one variable per (j,s,d) triple)
decision_variables.source_symbol: Q index k mapped to inds[k] = {"j","s","d"}

decision_variables.name: y_{(j,j1,s,d,d1)}
decision_variables.meaning: auxiliary variable from the Rosenberg quadratization of the cubic track-occupancy (HOBO) term

objective.expression_plain: minimize the weighted total secondary delay: each selected delay d contributes (penalty_weight(j,s)/d_max)*d to the diagonal of Q. Linear in x; encoded as the diagonal of the QUBO Q matrix.

constraints.name: sum_to_one
constraints.indexed_over: j in J, s in S_j
constraints.expression_plain: each train leaves each station on its path with exactly one delay value (Eq. 41). Encoded as the squared penalty p_sum*(sum_d x - 1)^2 via P_sum, contributing -1 on the diagonal and +1 to off-diagonal pairs within the same (j,s) block.

constraints.name: minimal_headway
constraints.indexed_over: ordered train pairs (j,j1) sharing segment (s -> s_next) in Jd
constraints.expression_plain: two trains departing the same station toward the same next station must respect minimal headway; conflicting (d,d1) delay pairs are forbidden (Eq. 42). Encoded as pairwise quadratic penalty p_pair via P_headway returning 1.0 for conflicting pairs.

constraints.name: single_track_line
constraints.indexed_over: opposite-direction train pairs (j,j1) on a single track between (s,s1) in Josingle
constraints.expression_plain: trains running in opposite directions over a single-track segment may not occupy it simultaneously; conflicting delay pairs are forbidden (Eq. 43). Encoded as a symmetrized pairwise quadratic penalty p_pair via P_single_track_line.

constraints.name: minimal_stay
constraints.indexed_over: same train j, consecutive stations (sp -> s)
constraints.expression_plain: a train cannot depart a station earlier than its arrival plus running time plus minimal stop; delay pairs violating this are forbidden (Eq. 44). Encoded as a symmetrized pairwise quadratic penalty p_pair via P_minimal_stay.

constraints.name: rolling_stock_circulation
constraints.indexed_over: turnaround train pairs (j,j1) at station s1 in Jround
constraints.expression_plain: an incoming train must arrive and be prepared before the outgoing train (reusing the same rolling stock) departs; conflicting delay pairs are forbidden (Eq. 45). Encoded as a symmetrized pairwise quadratic penalty p_pair via P_rolling_stock_circulation.

constraints.name: switch_occupation
constraints.indexed_over: train pairs (jp,jpp) sharing a switch s in Jswitch
constraints.expression_plain: two trains may not occupy a common switch within the reservation time tau^res of each other; conflicting delay pairs are forbidden (Eq. 46). Encoded as a pairwise quadratic penalty p_pair via P_switch_occupation.

constraints.name: track_occupation
constraints.indexed_over: train pairs (j,j1) sharing a track at station s in Jtrack, delays (d,d1)
constraints.expression_latex: \text{(originally cubic, Eq. 53) reduced via Rosenberg: } x_a x_b - 2 y (x_a + x_b) + 3 y \;\text{and quadratic part}
constraints.expression_plain: single track at a station can hold a limited number of trains: a train may only enter once a preceding train has released the track. Originally a cubic (HOBO) condition (Eq. 53); the cubic part is reduced to quadratic via a Rosenberg decomposition (P_Rosenberg_decomposition) introducing auxiliary y variables, plus a quadratic part (P_track_occupation_condition_quadratic_part). Weighted by p_pair (quadratic part) and p_qubic (Rosenberg).

instances.name: DWave_problem (default / rerouted)
instances.format: python class (taus, trains_timing, trains_paths, p_sum/p_pair/p_qubic, d_max=10)
instances.size: 3 trains, 2 stations (S1,S2); default and rerouted variants

instances.name: precomputed Q matrices (npz)
instances.size: Qfile.npz, Qfile_r.npz, Qfile_enlarged.npz (test/files/), Qfile_5trains.npz (test/files/, ~930 KB) etc.; symmetric QUBO matrices

models.notes: QUADRATIC/HOBO MODEL, NOT A LINEAR MILP. This repository contains NO classical CPLEX/docplex/pulp/gurobipy linear formulation; requirements.txt lists only D-Wave Ocean / dimod / numpy / scipy (no MILP solver). The model is assembled directly as a symmetric QUBO Q matrix (make_Qubo, make_qubo.py:630-661): the objective is linear (delay minimization, on the Q diagonal) but every operational constraint is realized as a SQUARED penalty term (off-diagonal Q entries), not as a linear (in)equality passed to a solver. The constraint rows above record the genuinely-linear underlying constraints from the cited paper (Eqs. 41-46, 53) that each penalty enforces; the latex shows the forbidden-pair (x_a*x_b=0) penalty form actually present in code, because the source itself never materializes them as linear constraints. The track-occupation condition is originally CUBIC (HOBO, Eq. 53) and is quadratized via a Rosenberg decomposition with auxiliary binary variables (P_Rosenberg_decomposition). Equation numbers (41-46, 53) refer to the cited paper; eq. 18 is also referenced inline (DW_example.py:66). The commented-out imports in Qfile_solve.py:11-14 (generate_ham_ilp, ilp_helpers, generate_ham_edge, edge_helpers) and a stray bytecode test/__pycache__/test_linear_solver_large.cpython-39-pytest-6.2.5.pyc reference an ILP/edge formulation, but NONE of those source modules exist in this repository; the leftover .pyc's strings reference only QUBO functions (make_Q, p_sum, p_pair, energy), so no classical linear model could be extracted.

extraction_notes: Confidence high for what IS present (the QUBO/HOBO construction in railway_solvers/make_qubo.py, fully read and traced) and for the negative finding that no classical linear/MILP model exists. The mathematical symbols, equation numbers (41-46, 53) and constraint meanings are taken from the docstrings in make_qubo.py which cite the paper (Domino, Kundu, Salehi, Krawiec, 'Quadratic and Higher-Order Unconstrained Binary Optimization of Railway Rescheduling for Quantum Computing', Quantum Information Processing 21:337, 2022, DOI 10.1007/s11128-022-03670-y, confirmed in README.md:131-134; the code also cites arXiv:2107.03234 at make_qubo.py:10). The DOI is taken verbatim from the README and is NOT inferred. The constraints are recorded once each as the underlying linear operational condition, annotated with the squared-penalty/quadratic encoding actually found in code, per the task instruction to capture only what is genuinely linear and to flag quadratic/QUBO nature honestly. No constraint was linearized by invention. Instances are referenced by path (inputs/DW_example.py, files/, test/files/), never inlined. License is Apache-2.0 (LICENSE, copied verbatim to sidecar).
